title: Kangaroo Level 1-2 - Practice Set 11: Logic and Puzzles
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: How many stars are drawn in the picture below?
questions.image_description: Four yellow stars in a row.
questions.solution: Count each star one by one: 1, 2, 3, 4. There are 4 stars.

questions.question_text: What number comes when you add 3 and 5 together?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='#eef'><text x='100' y='40' text-anchor='middle' font-size='28' font-family='Arial' fill='#333' stroke='none'>3 + 5 = ?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Expression 3 + 5 = ?

questions.question_text: Which of these shapes has exactly three sides?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 250 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><circle cx='25' cy='30' r='18'/><rect x='60' y='12' width='36' height='36'/><polygon points='125,12 108,48 142,48'/><polygon points='175,12 195,22 190,48 160,48 155,22'/><polygon points='225,15 245,30 225,45 205,30'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Circle, square, triangle, pentagon, rhombus.

questions.question_text: Look at the pattern: circle, square, circle, square, circle. What comes next?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='#e6f2ff'><circle cx='20' cy='25' r='12'/><rect x='48' y='13' width='24' height='24'/><circle cx='100' cy='25' r='12'/><rect x='128' y='13' width='24' height='24'/><circle cx='180' cy='25' r='12'/><text x='220' y='32' font-size='24' fill='#333' stroke='none'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Alternating circles and squares, then a question mark.
questions.solution: The pattern alternates circle, square. After the fifth item (circle), a square comes next.

questions.question_text: Mia has 4 red balloons and 2 blue balloons. How many balloons does she have in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='#ffcccc'><circle cx='20' cy='25' r='10'/><circle cx='50' cy='25' r='10'/><circle cx='80' cy='25' r='10'/><circle cx='110' cy='25' r='10'/><circle cx='160' cy='25' r='10' fill='#cce0ff'/><circle cx='190' cy='25' r='10' fill='#cce0ff'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Four red balloons and two blue balloons.

questions.question_text: Among these four houses, the numbers are 12, 9, 15, and 6. Which house has the smallest number?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='#fff'><text x='20' y='32' font-size='22' fill='#333' stroke='none'>9</text><text x='60' y='32' font-size='22' fill='#333' stroke='none'>3</text><text x='100' y='32' font-size='22' fill='#333' stroke='none'>7</text><text x='140' y='32' font-size='22' fill='#333' stroke='none'>5</text><text x='180' y='32' font-size='22' fill='#333' stroke='none'>6</text></svg>
questions.image_description: The numbers 9, 3, 7, 5, 6.

questions.question_text: Tom is taller than Sam. Sam is taller than Leo. Who is the shortest?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f5f5dc'><rect x='20' y='10' width='30' height='60'/><text x='35' y='78' font-size='10' fill='#333' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle'>Tom</text><rect x='80' y='25' width='30' height='45'/><text x='95' y='78' font-size='10' fill='#333' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle'>Sam</text><rect x='140' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><text x='155' y='78' font-size='10' fill='#333' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle'>Leo</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three bars showing Tom tallest, Sam middle, Leo shortest.
questions.solution: Tom > Sam > Leo. So Leo is the shortest.

questions.question_text: How many corners does a square have?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='#e0f0e0'><rect x='20' y='10' width='60' height='60'/><circle cx='20' cy='10' r='3' fill='#c00'/><circle cx='80' cy='10' r='3' fill='#c00'/><circle cx='20' cy='70' r='3' fill='#c00'/><circle cx='80' cy='70' r='3' fill='#c00'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A square with its four corners marked with red dots.

questions.question_text: What number goes in the empty box so that the equation is true?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='#eef'><text x='10' y='38' font-size='24' fill='#333' stroke='none'>7 + </text><rect x='60' y='15' width='30' height='30' fill='#fff'/><text x='100' y='38' font-size='24' fill='#333' stroke='none'> = 12</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Equation 7 + [box] = 12.

questions.question_text: How many small squares are used to make the picture below?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='#dde7f5'><rect x='10' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='10' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='70' y='30' width='20' height='20'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A shape made from small squares: 3 on top, 4 on bottom.
questions.solution: 3 squares on top + 4 squares on the bottom = 7 small squares.

questions.question_text: The numbers follow a rule: 2, 4, 6, 8, ?. What is the next number?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='#fff'><text x='20' y='32' font-size='22' fill='#333' stroke='none'>2</text><text x='60' y='32' font-size='22' fill='#333' stroke='none'>4</text><text x='100' y='32' font-size='22' fill='#333' stroke='none'>6</text><text x='140' y='32' font-size='22' fill='#333' stroke='none'>8</text><text x='180' y='32' font-size='22' fill='#333' stroke='none'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Sequence 2, 4, 6, 8, ?.

questions.question_text: Four children stand in a line. Ana is first. Ben is right behind Ana. Cara is last. Dan is between Ben and Cara. Who is third in the line?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='#fce'><circle cx='30' cy='25' r='12'/><circle cx='80' cy='25' r='12'/><circle cx='130' cy='25' r='12'/><circle cx='180' cy='25' r='12'/><text x='30' y='48' font-size='10' fill='#333' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle'>1</text><text x='80' y='48' font-size='10' fill='#333' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle'>2</text><text x='130' y='48' font-size='10' fill='#333' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle'>3</text><text x='180' y='48' font-size='10' fill='#333' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle'>4</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Four positions labeled 1, 2, 3, 4.
questions.solution: Order is Ana, Ben, Dan, Cara. The third is Dan.

questions.question_text: A pencil costs 6 coins and an eraser costs 2 coins. How many more coins does the pencil cost than the eraser?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='#ffe8a3'><rect x='10' y='22' width='80' height='10'/><polygon points='90,17 105,27 90,37'/><text x='40' y='52' font-size='10' fill='#333' stroke='none'>Pencil: 6</text><rect x='130' y='22' width='40' height='20' fill='#ffd'/><text x='145' y='58' font-size='10' fill='#333' stroke='none'>Eraser: 2</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A pencil labeled 6 and an eraser labeled 2.

questions.question_text: Which figure shows exactly one triangle and one circle?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='#eaf7ea'><g><polygon points='15,45 30,15 45,45'/><circle cx='60' cy='30' r='10'/><text x='38' y='58' font-size='9' fill='#333' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle'>A</text></g><g transform='translate(80,0)'><polygon points='15,45 30,15 45,45'/><polygon points='55,45 70,15 85,45'/><text x='45' y='58' font-size='9' fill='#333' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle'>B</text></g><g transform='translate(160,0)'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='50' cy='30' r='10'/><text x='35' y='58' font-size='9' fill='#333' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle'>C</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Option A: triangle + circle. Option B: two triangles. Option C: two circles.
questions.solution: Only Figure A has exactly one triangle and exactly one circle.

questions.question_text: A box has 10 apples. If 3 apples are taken out, how many apples are still inside the box?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 180 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0d0d0'><rect x='20' y='20' width='80' height='50' fill='#d9b28a'/><circle cx='35' cy='35' r='5' fill='#c33'/><circle cx='50' cy='35' r='5' fill='#c33'/><circle cx='65' cy='35' r='5' fill='#c33'/><circle cx='80' cy='35' r='5' fill='#c33'/><circle cx='35' cy='55' r='5' fill='#c33'/><circle cx='50' cy='55' r='5' fill='#c33'/><circle cx='65' cy='55' r='5' fill='#c33'/><circle cx='130' cy='30' r='5' fill='#c33'/><circle cx='150' cy='30' r='5' fill='#c33'/><circle cx='140' cy='50' r='5' fill='#c33'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A box with apples inside and three apples outside.

questions.question_text: A cat, a dog, and a rabbit sit in a row. The dog is not first. The rabbit is last. Who is first?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='#fffbe6'><rect x='20' y='15' width='40' height='30'/><rect x='100' y='15' width='40' height='30'/><rect x='180' y='15' width='40' height='30'/><text x='40' y='55' font-size='10' fill='#333' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle'>1st</text><text x='120' y='55' font-size='10' fill='#333' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle'>2nd</text><text x='200' y='55' font-size='10' fill='#333' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle'>3rd</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three empty boxes labeled 1st, 2nd, 3rd.
questions.solution: The rabbit is 3rd and the dog is not 1st, so the dog is 2nd. The cat is 1st.

questions.question_text: In a basket there are red, green, and yellow apples. There are 3 red apples, 2 more green than red, and 1 fewer yellow than green. How many apples are in the basket altogether?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='#e8f0e8'><rect x='20' y='20' width='180' height='50' fill='#d9b28a'/><circle cx='40' cy='40' r='6' fill='#c33'/><circle cx='55' cy='40' r='6' fill='#c33'/><circle cx='70' cy='40' r='6' fill='#c33'/><circle cx='95' cy='40' r='6' fill='#4a4'/><circle cx='110' cy='40' r='6' fill='#4a4'/><circle cx='125' cy='40' r='6' fill='#4a4'/><circle cx='140' cy='40' r='6' fill='#4a4'/><circle cx='155' cy='40' r='6' fill='#4a4'/><circle cx='170' cy='40' r='6' fill='#ee4'/><circle cx='185' cy='40' r='6' fill='#ee4'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A basket holding 3 red, 5 green, and 4 yellow apples.

questions.question_text: How many triangles can you count in the picture below?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0e8ff'><polygon points='60,10 10,90 110,90'/><line x1='60' y1='10' x2='60' y2='90'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A large triangle with a line from the top vertex to the middle of the bottom side.
questions.solution: Left small triangle, right small triangle, and the whole big triangle. That is 3 triangles.

questions.question_text: Shapes appear in order: triangle, circle, square, triangle, circle, square, and so on. What is the 10th shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 280 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='#e6f7ff'><polygon points='15,38 5,12 25,12'/><circle cx='45' cy='25' r='10'/><rect x='65' y='15' width='20' height='20'/><polygon points='105,38 95,12 115,12'/><circle cx='135' cy='25' r='10'/><rect x='155' y='15' width='20' height='20'/><text x='200' y='32' font-size='18' fill='#333' stroke='none'>...</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Repeating pattern of triangle, circle, square.
questions.solution: The pattern repeats every 3 shapes. Positions 1,4,7,10 are triangles, so the 10th is a triangle.

questions.question_text: Lena has 15 stickers. She gives 4 to her sister and 3 to her friend. How many stickers does Lena have left?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='#fff0f5'><text x='10' y='38' font-size='20' fill='#333' stroke='none'>15 - 4 - 3 = ?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Expression 15 - 4 - 3 = ?

questions.question_text: Each kangaroo eats 2 carrots a day. How many carrots do 4 kangaroos eat in one day?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 70' stroke='currentColor' fill='#d9b28a'><ellipse cx='30' cy='35' rx='18' ry='22'/><ellipse cx='90' cy='35' rx='18' ry='22'/><ellipse cx='150' cy='35' rx='18' ry='22'/><ellipse cx='210' cy='35' rx='18' ry='22'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Four kangaroo silhouettes.
questions.solution: 4 kangaroos times 2 carrots each equals 8 carrots.

questions.question_text: A ladybug climbs up 4 steps, then climbs down 1 step, then climbs up 3 more. How many steps up from the start is it now?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 160 120' stroke='currentColor' fill='#eee'><rect x='10' y='100' width='140' height='10'/><rect x='10' y='85' width='120' height='10'/><rect x='10' y='70' width='100' height='10'/><rect x='10' y='55' width='80' height='10'/><rect x='10' y='40' width='60' height='10'/><rect x='10' y='25' width='40' height='10'/><rect x='10' y='10' width='20' height='10'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A staircase of 7 steps going up to the left.

questions.question_text: Anna read 7 pages on Monday, 5 pages on Tuesday, and 8 pages on Wednesday. On which day did she read the most pages?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='#cfe2f3'><rect x='20' y='30' width='30' height='60'/><rect x='70' y='50' width='30' height='40'/><rect x='120' y='20' width='30' height='70'/><text x='35' y='98' font-size='10' fill='#333' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle'>Mon</text><text x='85' y='98' font-size='10' fill='#333' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle'>Tue</text><text x='135' y='98' font-size='10' fill='#333' stroke='none' text-anchor='middle'>Wed</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Bar chart with Monday 7, Tuesday 5, Wednesday 8.

questions.question_text: Three friends share 9 cookies equally. How many cookies does each friend get?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='#ffe0b3'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='45' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='70' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='110' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='135' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='160' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='200' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='225' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='250' cy='30' r='8'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Nine cookies grouped into three sets of three.
questions.solution: 9 divided into 3 equal groups gives 3 cookies each. (9 ÷ 3 = 3.)
